$comment: Pinned, SHA-verified HarfBuzz SOURCE release tarball for the M7 runtime-UI text shaper (src/packages/ui/text/, issue #237 / R-UI-005; a8 text-shaping). SINGLE source of truth for the pin — read by cmake/ContextHarfBuzz.cmake (string(JSON)) which fetches + verifies (cmake/ContextDownload.cmake) + extracts + builds the AMALGAMATED single translation unit src/harfbuzz.cc into the `harfbuzz` static target (DECISION-a7a8-deps.md: 'HarfBuzz: use the amalgamated single-TU src/harfbuzz.cc'). Like FreeType (and unlike the MSVC/Clang-ABI prebuilts V8/wgpu-native/CEF/wasmtime), HarfBuzz is portable C++ built FROM SOURCE here, so it compiles under the local Strawberry-GCC dev gate AND all three CI build legs (and the Emscripten web target) with no prebuilt and no toggle. The amalgamated TU pulls only HarfBuzz's built-in OpenType shaper + its bundled Unicode Character Database (hb-ucd.cc) — NO external Unicode/ICU/glib/FreeType provider is enabled (no HAVE_* defines), so no transitive dependency enters the shipped engine and the FreeType<->HarfBuzz circular option stays severed (FreeType built with FT_DISABLE_HARFBUZZ; R-SEC-006). The official meson-dist release tarball is byte-stable (an uploaded release asset, not a regenerated git-archive); the pin below is that artifact's SHA-256. Upstream COPYING is vendored verbatim under third_party/harfbuzz/ (NOTICE flow-down); tools/license-allowlist.json records 'harfbuzz':'MIT-Modern-Variant' ('Old MIT' — added to allowed_licenses).
